title: transport: port parse errors omit the bad value
description:
In core/src/transport.rs, both parse_proxy_addr (line 434) and parse_relay_addr (line 456) produce error messages that do not include the string that failed to parse:

  .context("proxy URL port is not a valid number")
  .context("relay URL port is not a valid number")

When these errors surface, the user sees no indication of what the bad input was. Should include port_str in the message, e.g.:

  .with_context(|| format!("proxy URL port '{}' is not a valid u16", port_str))

This matters in practice: a user who accidentally passes socks5://host:port/path would see the port parsing fail on the path component with no indication of what was wrong.
